figures.caption: Figure 1: Schematic representation of the Next-Generation Reservoir Computing (or standard NVAR) framework showing deterministic delay embedding and polynomial feature generation.
Figure 2: Schematic representation of the Adaptive NVAR framework showcasing the learnable MLP nonlinear mapping and the unified skip-connection optimization architecture.
Figure 3: Sample SST snapshot from the Copernicus Marine GLORYS12V1 reanalysis dataset used in this study
Figure 4: Explained variance spectrum of the PCA decomposition computed from the SST training dataset. The cumulative explained variance exceeds 99% within the first 10 principal components.
Figure 5: Temporal evolution of the leading principal component (PC1) obtained from the PCA decomposition.
Table 1: Explained variance ratios of the leading PCA modes computed from the SST training dataset.
Table 2: Hyperparameter search ranges used in the TPE optimization method.
Table 3: Optimal hyperparameter configurations and optimization time obtained using TPE optimization.
Table 4: Horizon-wise reconstructed SST forecast errors for standard NVAR and Adaptive NVAR models on the test dataset. Best results for each metric are indicated with bold letters.
Figure 6: Comparison between the true SST field, the SST field reconstructed by the standard NVAR model, and the corresponding absolute prediction error over the forecasting horizon.
Figure 7: Comparison between the true SST field, the SST field reconstructed by the proposed Adaptive NVAR model, and the corresponding absolute prediction error over the forecasting horizon.
